_note: index_url = listing/archive page Playwright harvests. verify_listing=true means confirm during canary that this URL actually lists dated articles (some are editorial guesses). All Concepts sources extract fully (no Flow in this stream).

sources.name: Chatham House
sources._note: un-parked 2026-07-08. /publications was TWO problems, not one: (1) Cloudflare flags the browser context after the index visit, same as ARK (fresh_context_per_article would have fixed this alone) — but (2) even with that fixed, /publications's static links are mostly topic/nav hub pages, not real report pages (confirmed: only 125-132 word stubs recovered). Checked /rss-feeds for a real listing instead: chathamhouse.org exposes 4 RSS feeds (What's new / Events / Expert comments / News releases), NONE linked from /publications itself. 'Expert comment' (path/83/feed.xml) is the clean one — 50 items, full HTML body inline in <description> (same shape as Damodaran's Blogger feed), 664-1537 words each (avg 1040), zero thin stubs, spanning 2026-05-14 to 2026-07-08 (~1/day cadence). Fetched via plain urllib (same as collect_source_rss()) with no Cloudflare challenge at all — this sidesteps the block entirely rather than working around it, so it is NOT residential-only: works from any IP, including the cloud cron. residential_only removed accordingly.

sources.name: Aswath Damodaran
sources._note: un-parked 2026-07-08: Blogger CAPTCHA ('unusual traffic') was hit on per-article-page navigation (Playwright visiting each post), not on the blog itself. The Blogger RSS feed (?alt=rss) returns the FULL post HTML inline in <description> with no CAPTCHA and no per-article page visit at all, so this is a config-level fetch_mode switch (scripts/collect_concepts.py collect_source_rss()), not a residential/bot-workaround. The 14 CAPTCHA-junk raw records collected 2026-07-08 pre-fix will be overwritten with real content on re-collection (same URL -> same record_id).

sources.name: ARK Invest Research
sources._note: un-parked 2026-07-08. Root cause of dropped_short=15/day was NOT teaser-only content and NOT IP-blocking: ARK's Cloudflare check flags the BROWSER CONTEXT once it has visited the index/listing page, and every article visited afterward in that SAME context then hard-fails a challenge that never clears (tested up to 20s+ wait). A brand-new context visiting the same article URL directly passes in ~3s with full real text. fresh_context_per_article makes collect_source() open one throwaway Playwright context per article for this source instead of reusing the shared page. Confirmed: 8/15 candidates kept on a --days 90 test run (0 dropped_short, vs 15/15 before).

sources.name: Hussman Funds Commentary
sources._note: index_url fixed 2026-07-08: old .../comment.html 404s; this path confirmed live, current (latest entry 2026-06-01 as of the fix). article_path_re added same day: memo slugs (e.g. /comment/mc260601/) have no year segment, no 4+ digit doc-id, and no hyphens, so the generic looks_like_article() detector never matched them — the URL fix alone was NOT sufficient; without this override the source would still have silently yielded 0 candidates forever.

sources.name: SSRN
sources.skip_reason: LISTING BUG FIXED 2026-07-08 but a HARDER block remains underneath. Fixed: index_url now points at the ERN (Economics Research Network) category page, which lists real papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=NNNNNNN links directly (no 2-level crawl needed) — added article_query_re since abstract_id lives in the query string, not the path, so the generic path-based detector could never match it. Confirmed via a direct collect_source() test: 55 real abstract_id candidates found (vs 15 static category links before), 0 dropped_old. BUT: papers.ssrn.com itself serves a Cloudflare interactive challenge ('Just a moment...') on every single abstract page, tested with a completely FRESH browser context (not the ARK-style context-flagging pattern — this fails even cold, first visit, no prior navigation) and with waits up to 20s+. This is a materially harder block than ARK's context-based flag; the fresh_context_per_article fix that recovered ARK does NOT help here. Worth a one-off test once/if a residential IP is available (uncertain whether IP reputation is even the driver here vs an interactive JS/proof-of-work challenge), but not assumed to be residential-recoverable.
sources.revisit_route: cloudflare_interactive_challenge — residential test unresolved; also revive with NBER when the Science line is built (grep future_line=Science)
sources._note: parked 2026-07-08 during daily-stream health sweep; skip_reason corrected same day (cloud_ip_blocked was wrong) and again same day after the listing fix surfaced the real papers.ssrn.com Cloudflare block underneath.
